observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

     1. Explain why the noted partially and fully completed  projects  were
        still not recorded in the books of accounts;


     2. Require the Municipal Engineer to  accomplish  the  inspection  and
        acceptance report and the certificate of completion duly signed  by
        the concerned officials, contractor and Head of  the  Agency  after
        final inspection was conducted;


      3.  Ensure  to  furnish  copies  of  monitoring/progress  reports  and
         certificates  of  completion  to  the  Office  of   the   Municipal
         Accountant for the processing of partial and final payments; and


      4. Direct the Municipal Accountant to:


        (a) use the Construction  in  Progress  account  to  recognize  the
        accumulated cost or other appropriate value of buildings and  other
        structures which are  still  in  the  process  of  construction  in
        accordance with Chapter 3, Vol. 3 and Chapter 7, Vol. 1 of the  GAM
        for LGUs; and


        (b) cause the reclassification of accounts  from  the  CIP  to  the
        appropriate Buildings, Other  Structures,  Land  Improvements,  and
        Infrastructure Asset accounts to take up the completed projects  in
        the books of  accounts  to  fairly  present  the  accounts  in  the
        financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

      1.  Secure and keep copies of all agreements i.e., MOA about the funds
        received from NGAs and other LGUs  because  they  can  be  used  by
        Management in decision-making with regard to the conditions of  the
        donation considering the materiality of the amounts involved;


      2. Pay importance to the  Memorandum/Trust  Agreements  and/or  Policy
        Guidelines on the release and use of the funds transferred from the
        National  Government  since   they   prescribe   the   step-by-step
        procedures and documentary requirements to be complied by  LGUs  to
        allow a smooth implementation  of  the  project  and  avoid  future
        problems that might affect the continuity of project implementation
        such as land titling and/or right-of-way acquisition issues;


      3.  Require the Municipal Accountant to:


        a)  Record in  the  Trust  Fund  books  all  ongoing  or  partially
           completed projects using the Construction  in  Progress  account
           and transfer  the  completed  projects  into  their  proper  PPE
           account for fair presentation in the financial statements. While
           the projects were not officially turned over yet by  the  source
           agencies  and  pending  compliance  of  any  lacking   documents
           relative to project completion and acceptance,  the  assets  may
           remain in the Trust Fund  books.  However,  require  the  source
           agency to have the projects officially turned over to  the  LGU,
           if warranted;


        b) Observe the proposed accounting entries illustrated in the IPSAS
           and GAM involving fund transfers to  make  use  of  the  correct
           accounts, prevent any material under/overstatement  of  the  PPE
           and Due to NGAs accounts at year-end, and properly  present  the
           actual nature of transactions; and


      4. Require the offices concerned to submit completely to the Municipal
        Accountant the necessary Disbursement Vouchers  and  its  supporting
        documents under the Trust Fund such  as  Inspection  and  Acceptance
        Reports and certificates of completion and/or acceptance to  support
        the complete and timely recording of the assets  and  other  related
        accounts in the trust fund books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

      1. Secure and keep copies of Memorandum/Trust Agreements and/or Policy
         Guidelines on the release and use of  the  funds  transferred  from
         National Government Agencies because they can be used by Management
         in  decision-making  regarding  the  conditions  of  the   donation
         considering the materiality of the amounts involved.  The  step-by-
         step procedures and documentary requirements to be complied by LGUs
         from  recording,  project  implementation  up  to  monitoring   are
         prescribed in these documents; and

   2. Require the Municipal Accountant to:


        a)  Record as asset in the Trust Fund books the procured laboratory
           equipment for fair presentation in the financial statements.  It
           may remain in the Trust Fund books if it’s not officially turned
           over yet by the source agency  and  pending  compliance  of  any
           lacking documents relative to project completion and acceptance.
           For this and other forthcoming  donated  projects,  require  the
           source agency for an official turnover to the LGU; and


        b) Observe the proposed accounting entries illustrated in the PPSAS
           and GAM involving fund transfers to  make  use  of  the  correct
           accounts, prevent any material under/overstatement  of  the  PPE
           and Due to NGAs accounts at year-end, and properly  present  the
           actual nature of transactions.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

     1. Direct the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with  the  Inventory
        and Appraisal Committee, to (1)  issue  a  written  report  on  the
        extent and cost of the damage  to  the  flood  control  system  and
        officially declare it unserviceable and/or beyond repair,  and  (2)
        assess other properties that might have also been  damaged  by  the
        recent calamities and submit a written report on the assessment.


     2. Direct the Municipal GSO to immediately (1) submit a written notice
        of loss of  property  (i.e.,  the  flood  control  system)  to  the
        Municipal Mayor and the Audit Team, and  (2)  request  relief  from
        accountability due to fortuitous event from the COA  following  the
        procedural flow in Appendix B.


     3.  Subsequently,  direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  record  the
        derecognition of the lost or damaged asset following Section 268 of
        the GAM for LGUs Volume I.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:

     1. Consider enrollment in the AGDB’s online facilities and direct  the
        Municipal Treasurer and  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  facilitate
        compliance with the requirements for the enrollment.


     2. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to practice sending  written  notice
        to the payees one month before the check becomes stale as  required
        in Section 104 of GAM for LGUs, and  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
        make prompt adjustments in the books following  the  provisions  in
        the LGAM.


     3. Direct the Municipal Accountant to (1)  immediately  and  regularly
        prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation  Statements  for  all  bank
        accounts of the Municipality and submit the same to the  Office  of
        the Auditor; (2)  be  conscious  of  the  prescribed  timelines  of
        submission to avoid penalties that may be imposed.

Recommendations:

      We recommended that:


     1. The Local Chief Executive  to  direct  the  reconstitution  of  the
        Inventory and Appraisal Committee  to  identify  and  appraise  all
        properties  which  cost  cannot  be  reliably  determined  due   to
        unavailability of financial records.


     2. The Inventory and Appraisal  Committee  to  dutifully  conduct  the
        annual physical count of all  the  property,  plant  and  equipment
        owned  by  the  Municipality  to  establish   the   existence   and
        correctness of PPE account balances;


     3. The GSO to promptly prepare the complete RPCPPE as  per  regulation
        stated above, indicating the details per  inventoried  item  as  to
        property description, property number, unit of measure, acquisition
        date,  and  costs,  balance  per  card,   balance   on   hand   and
        status/condition at the time of inspection;


     4. The GSO to reconcile RPCPPE with  the  records  of  the  Accounting
        Office to establish the correct amount of the PPE in the  financial
        statements and submit it to the Auditor concerned  not  later  than
        January 31 of the following year.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct:

     a) The Municipal Accountant to adopt the perpetual inventory system in
        recording the purchases of supplies and materials as  provided  for
        under Sections 114 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I;


     b) The GSO to require the different Municipal Departments/Offices to:


         •  prepare  a  summary  of  supplies  and  materials  issued   for
           consolidation by the GSO and submission to  the  Office  of  the
           Municipal Accountant as required under Section 121 of  the  NGAS
           Manual, Volume I; and



         • conduct physical inventory of supplies and materials,  by  type,
           as required under Section 124 of the same Manual.

     c) The GSO and Accounting Office oversee the conduct of the  inventory
        process regularly to ensure control, monitoring, and accounting  of
        all inventory items and accurately report the balances as presented
        in the Municipality’s financial statements.

Recommendations:

      We recommended for the Municipal Accountant to:


     1. exhaust all means to support the  balances  of  the  above  dormant
        accounts to identify and locate the debtors. Once identified,  send
        demand letters for them to settle their long-outstanding accounts;


     2. exert all efforts and if the  result  of  preceding  recommendation
        renders futile, follow the guidelines outlined in COA Circular 2016-
        005 to request for the write-off of these accounts, if warranted;


     3. use the proper classification of accounts per COA Circular No. 2015-
        009 which prescribed  the  Revised  Chart  of  Accounts  for  Local
        Government Units under PPSAS; and


     4. set up the related allowance for impairment and the impairment loss
        on long outstanding  Loans  Receivable  –  Others  based  on  sound
        judgment upon the data available.

     1. account all unutilized 30% QRF and DRRM-MOOE  from  years  2015  to
        2018 and transfer it from the general  fund  to  trust  fund  books
        using the following entries:

|Book   |Dr./Cr.|Account Code|Account Title      |Amount    |
|Gen.   |Dr.    |30101010    |Government Equity  |xxx       |
|Fund   |       |            |                   |          |
|       |Cr.    |10102010    |Cash in Bank, LCCA |xxx       |
|Trust  |Dr.    |10102010    |Cash in Bank, LCCA |xxx       |
|Fund   |       |            |                   |          |
|       |Cr.    |20401020    |Trust              |Xxx       |
|       |       |            |Liability-DRRM     |          |

        sources. This is  to  ensure  clear  monitoring  of  funds  and  to
        properly account any unutilized amount after the five-year  period.

        after subsequent enactment by Local Sanggunian.

     3. reconcile the outstanding balance of Trust Liabilities-DRRM to  the
        amounts presented in the utilization report and perform adjustments
        to the said account if found appropriate.

     4. submit subsidiary ledger of Trust Liabilities-DRRM to MDRRM Officer
        every year end to monitor and evaluate the  balances  of  the  said
        account. Any unutilized balance  of  DRRM  budget  (except  capital
        outlay) at year-end that is not transferred to  Trust  Liabilities-
        DRRM, shall be notified  by  the  DRRM  Officer  to  the  Municipal
        Account for necessary adjustments.

      We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:

     1. instruct the Municipal Accountant to allow the office  of  DRRM  to
        use the unexpended MOOE of 70% mitigation funds in Trust Liability-
        DRRM account and other continuing appropriations in compliance with
        Section 4.4 of COA Circular 2012-002. Also, restrict the unexpended
        30% QRF reserved for any future calamity that might strike  in  the
        municipality. Separate the SL per year for 30% QRF and 70%  QRF  to
        account properly the unutilized funds pertaining to it.


     2. direct the DRRM  Officer  to  conduct  adequate  planning  so  that
        programmed projects/activities  under  LDRRM  funds  be  reasonably
        attained in the current year and to obtain optimum  utilization  of
        the available funds.


     3. ensure that unexpended balance of MOOE from the 70% mitigation fund
        of the current year be reprogrammed/re-appropriated in the  ensuing
        year and the planned PPAs be incorporated to LDRRMFIP  and  revised
        AIP.

Recommendations:

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:

     a. prepare a reconciliation schedule of reciprocal accounts  and  take
        up the necessary adjustments in the books  of  accounts  to  ensure
        that all reconciling items are resolved and adjusted  on  a  timely
        basis; and


     b. perform constant review and  appraisal  of  transactions  to  deter
        error and omissions.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:

     a. examine and verify  available  records  to  establish  the  correct
        amount of liability, especially those  items  whose  SAs  were  not
        specified in the SLs; and


     b.  immediately  return  the  excess/unexpended  funds  for  completed
        projects/program, to settle obligations to their  SAs  pursuant  to
        COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the Local Chief Executive  to  instruct  the  Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:

     a. promptly remit all the unremitted mandatory  obligations,  such  as
        GSIS,  Pag-Ibig,  and  Phil-health  to  avoid  unnecessary  penalty
        charges enforced  by  the  concerned  depository  agencies  to  the

municipality  under  “Loans  Payable”  (code   2-01-02-040)   account   and

Other Payables.

BIR within the prescribed period to avoid additional charges  or  penalties,
interest and possible misuse of funds.

        requirements for the segregation and storage of  solid  waste.  The
        solid waste containers shall be properly marked or  identified  for

        or  "special  waste,"  or  any  other  classification  as  may   be
        determined by the Commission. Establish/re-establish the  Materials
        Recovery  Facility  (MRF)  within  the  barangay  or   cluster   of
        barangays.


     2. conduct regular orientation to educate the waste  generators  about
        the importance of proper waste segregation. Require  the  MENRO  to
        closely monitor the implementation of the formulated 10-Year  Solid
        Waste Management Plan (2014-2023) of the municipality.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the concerned Municipal Officials to:


     1. review thoroughly the cited programs and projects of the  remaining
        fund of P58,840,489.59 and prioritize it to the assessed  needs  of
        the  locality  through  the  Municipal  Planning  and   Development
        Council.


     2. conduct adequate planning so that programmed projects in the AIP be
        reasonably  attained  in  the  current  year  to   obtain   optimum
        utilization of the 20% Development Fund. See to  it  that  projects
        that are unlikely  to  be  implemented  in  the  current  year  are
        excluded in the priority projects.

Recommendations:

      We recommended the MHRM Officer to:


     1. prioritize formulation hiring plan of casuals  and  job  orders  to
        strengthen internal controls pertaining  to  it.  Also,  department
        heads  and  officials  should  be  able  to  provide  a  reasonable

        their contracts and make  sure  that  such  are  clear,  known  and
        accepted by employees prior to rendition of services to  facilitate

     3. see to it that accomplishment reports should be evaluated vis-à-vis
        the terms/ services agreed upon in the contract.

Recommendations:

concerned to:

     1. observe the schedules on GAD Planning and Budgeting as provided  by
        PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC no. 2013-01, as amended by  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
        JMC 2016-01 to facilitate timely submission to the  Local  Planning
        and Development Office and the subsequent review and endorsement by
        the DILG Regional, Provincial, or City/ Municipal Office.


     2.  maximize  the  utilization  of   the   GAD   funds   through   the
        implementation of GAD-related programs and projects to  attain  the

        budget and utilization in all reports.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

We recommend the following:

   1. Re-activate Inventory and Appraisal Committee to identify and appraise
      all properties  whose  cost  cannot  be  reliably  determined  due  to
      unavailability of financial records. Require the committee to  prepare
      Appraisal Report  and  submit  it  to  the  Commission  on  Audit  for
      Technical Review and Inspection.


   2. Conduct the annual physical count  of  all  the  property,  plant  and
      equipment owned by the Municipality to  establish  the  existence  and
      correct the balances of PPE accounts.

   3. The GSO  should  immediately  issue  the  Acknowledgement  Receipt  of
      Equipment (ARE) to every  personnel  in  possession  or  in-charge  of
      Property, Plant and Equipment. Start with the newly procured Equipment
      in CY 2016 downwards until fully accomplished.


   4. Prepare promptly and completely the Report on the  Physical  Count  of
      PPE as per regulation stated above.
   5. Submit religiously the RPCPPE to the Auditor concerned not later  than
      January 31 of each year.


   6. The GSO and the Municipal Accountant should  completely  maintain  the
      prescribed Property  Cards  and  PPE  Ledger  Cards  respectively  and
      reconcile it with the RPCPPE to establish the correct  amount  of  the
      PPE in the financial statements.


      It is also noteworthy to say that accomplishing these  recommendations
is vital and significantly important as these would make  the  PPE  accounts
and the Financial Statements reliable, accurate and fairly presented.

Recommendations:

      We recommend the Municipal Accountant should:


     1. Analyze  and  review  thoroughly  the  accounts  in  the  financial
        statements for accurate presentation.  Ensure  loans  are  properly
        classified  into  their  Current  and   Non-Current   portions   in
        accordance with PD 1445 and PPSAS.


     2. Prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the  adjustment  of
        P6,000.00.   Reflect   the   corresponding   correct    Accumulated
        Depreciation and Depreciation (Prior Period Adjustment) that should
        have been recorded if the error was not committed. We recommend the
        journal entry below:

                                                  Dr.               Cr.
        Loans Payable – Domestic                         P 6,000.00
        Accumulated Depreciation -Eqpt                        XX
                 Equipment (or the approp. Asset acct)              P
        6,000.00
                             Prior             Period             adjustment
XX

Recommendations:

      We recommend the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to:


          1. Closely monitor the above cash advances,  send  demand  letters
             for the immediate settlement and/or refund thereof.

          2. Advise all employees concerned to settle/liquidate  their  cash
             advances within the prescribed period as stated above.

          3. Review  and  refrain  from  granting  cash  advances  to  those
             employees with unliquidated cash advance in the books.

Recommendations:

      We recommend the concerned Municipal Officials should:


        1. Review  thoroughly  the  cited  programs  and  projects  of  the
           remaining fund  of  P25,147,551.18  and  prioritize  it  to  the
           assessed needs of the locality through  the  Municipal  Planning
           and Development Council.

        2. Conduct adequate planning so that programmed projects in the AIP
           be reasonably attained in the current  year  to  obtain  optimum
           utilization of the 20% Development Fund.  Furthermore,  projects
           as embodied in the AIP must be clearly specified  and  submitted
           to COA for timely evaluation and review.

        3. Ensure unexpended  balance  of  the  current  year  be  promptly
           reprogrammed/re-appropriated in the ensuing year  as  prescribed
           in Sec 4 of DILG MC No. 97-30.

Recommendations:

      We recommend that:


     1. The Municipal Accountant maintain special accounts in  the  General
        Fund for the public utilities, economic enterprises and development
        projects of the  municipality  using  complete  set  of  subsidiary
        ledgers to ensure such fund had  been  appropriated  and  disbursed
        only for the purpose/s for which they are  intended,  and  to  have
        adequate and reliable information as to its assets, liabilities and
        equity.


     2. Separate financial statements  (Statement  of  Financial  Position,
        Statement of Financial Performance and Statement of Cash Flows)  be
        prepared which shall form part of  the  schedules  of  the  General
        Fund, and submitted regularly to COA as  provided  by  regulations.
        Refer to and follow the procedures provided in Sections 105 to  110
        of Chapter 6, NGAS Manual Volume 1.

Recommendations:


      We recommend the following:


   1. The LCE shall monitor the preparation of the LDRRMFIP and  every  year
      thereafter to serve as the main guide in the monitoring and evaluating
      the specific projects and activities for which the DRRM fund  must  be
      utilized.

   2. Ensure submission of required DRRM reports to the Audit Team and other
      oversight  agencies  within  the  deadline  set,  in  accordance  with
      pertinent laws and regulations mentioned above.

   3.  Ensure  the  proper  and  efficient  utilization   of   the   LDRRMF,
      particularly the 70%  allocation  with  details  as  to  projects  and
      activities to be funded. Further enhance  implementation  of  programs
      which are highly responsive to the four thematic areas of  the  LDRRMP
      in accordance with the NDRRMP.

   4. Require close  coordination  between  the  MDRRMO  and  the  Municipal
      Accountant’s Office to come up with accurate recording, monitoring and
      evaluation of DRRM projects, programs and activities  and  ensure  the
      transfer of the unexpended balances of the DRRMF-MOOE to Special Trust
      Fund for CYs 2015 and 2016 for  fair  presentation  of  the  Financial
      Statements.

Recommendation:

Treasurer to observe timeliness in the remittance of  the  provincial  share
of the SEF collection which is within 15 days  of  every  succeeding  month.
The Municipal  Treasurer  should  also  revisit  R.A.  5447  to  familiarize
his/her duties and responsibilities on the handling of SEF collections.

Recommendations:

      We  recommend  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  should  direct  the
Municipal Budget Officer in coordination with Local School Board  to  ensure
the proposed budget was properly balanced, reasonably distributed,  and  the
most priority expenses which is repairs and maintenance of school  buildings
and other facilities of public elementary and secondary  schools  as  stated
in Section 100 of RA 7160 were greatly considered.

      We also recommend  that  whatever  projects,  programs  or  activities
embodied in the budget should have been seriously  implemented  to  optimize
the utilization and accomplish the purpose  for  which  the  fund  has  been
created.

Recommendations:

      The Audit Team recommends the following:

     1. Formally create the Municipal  Environment  and  Natural  Resources
        Office (MENRO) and employ/appoint head of this office, who will  be
        responsible in supervising, implementing and monitoring the 10-year
        Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan of the municipality.


     2. Allocate reasonable budget for the newly created office to  perform
        its functions and accomplish targets/projects embodied in  the  10-
        year ESWM Plan and other matters concerning the office  and  employ
        additional labor force and equipment to  strengthen  its  operation
        efficiently and effectively.


     3. Intensify Information, Education and Communication (IEC) activities
        to enhance awareness on the role and responsibility of citizens  in
        solid waste management and environmental protection.

     4. Strictly implement segregation of solid wastes and establishment of
        “functional” material recovery facility in  all  barangays  of  the
        municipality.

     5. Re-activate/Convene the ESWMB to closely monitor  and  enforce  the
        implementation of the MSWMP and review it at least every 2 years or
        as the need arises as mandated by RA 9003.

Recommendations:

      We recommend that management:


     1. Be in strict adherence with rules and regulations in the submission
        of copies of perfected contracts  with  complete  basic  supporting
        documents as enumerated under Section 3.1.2 of COA  Circular  2009-
        001  and  Section  17.1  of  the  Revised  Implementing  Rules  and
        Regulations of RA 9184, whenever applicable.


     2. Submit all the required documents as enumerated under Section 3.1.2
        of COA Circular 2009-001 and under Section 9.1 of COA Circular 2012-
        001 for all types of procurement through public bidding.


     3. Strictly comply with RA 9184.

Recommendations:

      In order to foster public awareness and monitoring of the  procurement
process  and  implementation  of  perfected   and   awarded   infrastructure
contracts and other procurement activities of the Municipal  Government,  we
recommend the following:

   1. To require the Bids and Awards Committee to  prepare  the  Procurement
      Monitoring Report (PMR) for all types of procurements at  the  end  of
      each semester through an Executive Order and have it approved  by  the
      HOPE.

   2. Monitor the execution and the submission of  the  duly  approved  PMR,
      furnishing a copy to the Audit Team’s Office, to the GPPB  in printed
      and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end
      of each semester.

Recommendations:

      We recommend management to:

     1. Strictly adhere with rules and regulations  in  the  submission  of
        copies  of  perfected  contracts  with  complete  basic  supporting
        documents as enumerated in COA Circulars 2009-001 and 2012-001  and
        the provisions of Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of  RA
        9184, whenever applicable;

     2. Submit the lacking documents mentioned above;


     3. Review the contract and each of  all  the  documents  forming  part
        thereof for completeness, before submission to COA; and

     4. Request for inspection of procured vehicle  is  necessary  for  the
        team to request Technical Personnel from  the  Regional  Office  to
        handle the actual inspection.

Recommendations:

      We recommend to the management the following:

      1. Strict adherence to rules and  regulations  in  the  submission  of
copies of perfected contracts with complete basic  supporting  documents
as enumerated under Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular 2009-001 and Section 17.1

      3. Strict compliance of RA 9184, otherwise known as “An Act  Providing

      We recommended that the transaction  documents,  financial  statements
and supporting schedules, the monthly  Bank  Reconciliation  Statements  and

within the prescribed legal due dates.  The attached Annex  C-7  will  serve

The Municipal Agriculturist recovered the documents pertaining to the Plant Now, Pay Later Program of the Municipality implemented in the previous administration. He submitted to the Audit Team the copies of the MOA and List of Beneficiaries, along with his statement under oath that due to the droughts that hit the municipality in the prior years and in the recent years 2015 and 2016, the trees given to beneficiaries were severely damaged and were not sustained. With the foregoing supporting documents, the Accounting Office derecognized this Other Investments account amounting to P5,438,896.50 which remained dormant in the books for more than fifteen years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant should strictly adhere to the period of liquidation as stated in COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994, and immediately liquidate/remit transferred funds that intended purpose/projects are already completed. Furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the remittance/deposit slip, together with the corresponding Disbursement Voucher and Journal Entry Voucher.
We also recommended the Implementing Offices of the projects/programs to immediately report completed projects to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of complete documentation of the liquidations.
When the amount of impairment loss is determined as it is probable that the investment is impaired, we recommend the following entries: (5-05-04-990) Other Losses P xxx (1-02-05-991) Allow. for Impairment –Other Investments P xxx
If subsequently, derecognition is deemed necessary, record the following entries: (1-02-05-991) Allow. for Impairment–Other Investments P xxx (1-02-05-990) Other Investments P xxx
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adheres to all the recommendations of the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant already started returning the remaining balances of implemented projects to source agencies. However, for some old balances, she could not determine where to return the balances anymore due to lack of necessary data. She promised, however to continually update this account to ensure that all remaining balances are promptly returned to source agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: Require those persons responsible in the transactions suspended in audit to give preferential attention in settling the same for these may mature into disallowance. Direct the persons liable to immediately settle audit disallowances while still in the service to refrain from experiencing the difficulty of collecting the same in the case of employees’ retirement or separation from the service or transferred to other agencies. Oblige those employees who are still in the service but identified and determined as one or among the persons liable to settle the audit disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adheres to all the recommendations of the Audit Team. The Municipal officials concerned are already gathering the necessary documents to support their response to the Audit Team regarding the suspensions. The disallowances will be complied with, but one of the disallowances would be appealed by the person involved.

observations.recommendation: The audit team recommended that management direct the officers/personnel responsible to submit the copies of contracts/purchase orders together with complete supporting documents to the audit team within the reglementary period to facilitate timely review of the documents. It is also recommended to submit a written explanation on the delayed submission of the required documents cited in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated Feb. 12, 2009 and complete the documentation cited above.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adheres to all the recommendations of the Audit Team. The BAC, GSO and Mun. Accountant’s Office promised to coordinate with each other to comply with all the recommendation of the Audit Team especially regarding compliance and submission of necessary documents within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: The management should be in strict adherence with rules and regulations in the submission of copies of perfected contracts with complete basic supporting documents as enumerated under Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular 2009-001 and Section 17.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184, whenever applicable. Submit all the required documents as enumerated under Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular 2009-001 and under Section 9.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 for all types of procurement through public bidding. Strict compliance of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adheres to all the recommendations of the Audit Team. The BAC, GSO and the Municipal Accountant are in coordination to remit within the prescribed period the share of the Provincial Government of Davao del Sur, now Davao Occidental.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: To require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) for all types of procurements at the end of each semester and have it approved by the HOPE. To submit the duly approved PMR, furnishing a copy to the Audit Team’s Office, to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.
observations.managementAction: The LGU adheres to all recommendations in AAR. The GSO stated that his office already prepared the PMR for the first and second semesters of CY 2016. However, as of this writing, these semi-annual reports were not submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the immediate remittance of RPT shares to the PGDS as well as Barangay shares of all Barangays in the Municipality. Coordination between the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant is needed to efficiently address the issue on the delayed of remittances. Establish a system to improve the process.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adheres to all the recommendations of the Audit Team. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant are in coordination to remit within the prescribed period the share of the Provincial Government of Davao del Sur, now Davao Occidental.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management should instruct the OIC-MDRRMO, LDRRMC and all others concerned: to revisit the MDRRMP and ensure that the Plan presents the goals, objectives, expected outputs and performance indicators and measures consistent with the NDRRMP as well as the specific timelines for each activity and Plan’s period of coverage; to have the LDRRMP submitted to the SB through the LDRRMC and LDC together with the LDRRMFIP and other DRRM fund sources, if any, for review and subsequent monitoring and evaluation; to review the current LDRRMP and come up with revisions that would make it more aligned with the NDRRMP. Improve the Plan such that its activities and performance could be better monitored and evaluated.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adheres to all the recommendations of the Audit Team. The MDRRMO already revised the LDRRMP for CYs 2017-2019 to make it consistent with the NDRRMP. She also promised to probe on the purchase of seedlings in CY 2015 to enable her office to fully comply with the recommendations of the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: Evaluate carefully the goods for procurement for DRRM purposes with consideration to their necessity, shelf life, storage requirements, and the reasonableness of the quantities to be procured for stockpiling, if necessary, to ensure that such goods are utilized for the purpose for which the same were procured. Coordinate with barangay officials and secure list of qualified recipients (with available area) for the calamity assistance. Moreover, confirm with the qualified recipients if the assistance appropriately address their needs. Be cautious on the weather conditions provided by PAG-ASA especially on the forecast of El Niño Phenomenon and refrain from procuring any planting materials not suitable to be planted during dry season. Conduct monitoring/inspection of distributed planting materials to determine the status of survival and submit to COA for validation. Require concerned officials and employees to comply or submit written explanation/justification on observations enumerated above, in compliance with the rules and regulations set forth in RA 10121 and other pertinent provisions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adheres to all the recommendations of the Audit Team. The MDRRMO already revised the LDRRMP for CYs 2017-2019 to make it consistent with the NDRRMP. She also promised to probe on the purchase of seedlings in CY 2015 to enable her office to fully comply with the recommendations of the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive (LCE) and all others concerned should ensure that the essential elements in GAD planning are present or strengthened in the Municipality’s GPB. The LCE and GAD Focal Point System should initiate and assist the Local Sanggunian in the formulation of a GAD Code or ordinance. Ensure that the pertinent provisions of the GAD Code are integrated in local development plans as required per JMC 2013-01. The LCE should direct the GAD Focal Point System and other personnel concerned to strictly adhere to the provisions of JMC No. 2012-01 so that formulation, development, submission, implementation, monitoring, evaluation and accounting of results of the Municipality’s annual GPB and GAD Accomplishment Reports will be in order. We recommended further that the Municipality should conduct gender audit and mainstreaming and establish GAD database and sex-disaggregated data to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation as provided under Section 4.0 of PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adheres to all the recommendations of the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
1. Cause the immediate liquidation of the unexpended balances of the fund transfers made by the Department of Health and Department of Interior and Local Government in the amount of P1,075.18 and P6,197.60 respectively.
2. Follow-up the approval of the plans and specifications of the P12.2M fund transfer. Time is of the essence for the constituents longing to have a potable water system.
3.Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 94-013 on the rules and regulations in the grant, utilization and liquidations of the funds transferred to Implementing Agencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adheres to all the recommendations of the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant already returned the unexpended balances of the fund transfers made by the DOH and DILG. The downloaded fund from the DILG amounting to P12.2M was already started and is already on-going.

observations.recommendation: To fully achieve the purpose of the EWSMP, which is to make Malita a 90% garbage-free environment, we recommended the formal creation of Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office (MENRO) to focus on the execution of the strategies and plans embodied in the 10-year ESWMP. Furthermore, we recommended employing additional labor force and procurement of equipment to increase the scope of garbage collection so as to cater more households. Monitor the establishment/re-establishment of the Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) within the barangays or cluster of barangays for convenience in collection of non-recyclable materials and special wastes. Conduct regular orientation to educate the waste generators about the importance of proper waste segregation. Require the MENRO or ESWMB to closely monitor the implementation of the formulated 10 Year Solid Waste Management Plan (2014-2023).
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adheres to all the recommendations of the Audit Team. The MENRO-Designate coordinated with Barangay Officials for the re-establishment of MRFs in the barangay and municipal level. The 10-year plan is still subject to evaluation and deliberation of the National Solid Waste Commission therefore the ESWMB of the Municipality cannot yet institute revision of the Municipal ESWMP.

observations.recommendation: The team recommends that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant shall closely monitor the above cash advances, send demand letters for the immediate settlement and/or refund thereof. Advise all employees concerned whose cash advances were granted to settle/liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period as stated above. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant shall review and refrain from granting cash advances to those employees with unliquidated cash advance in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adheres to all the recommendations of the Audit Team. Advances granted to officers and employees in the prior years were already significantly liquidated by concerned individuals.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the transaction documents, financial statements and supporting schedules, the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and other related reports stated above be submitted to COA Audit Team within the prescribed time. The attached Annex C-3 will serve as guide on completeness and timely submission in compliance with each specific legal basis for submission of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adheres to all the recommendations of the Audit Team. The Mun. Treasurer’s Office and the Mun. Accountant’s Office are already in coordination for prompt submission of required reports to COA.

observations.recommendation: The management should limit the granting of cash advances to an accountable officer up to her approved maximum cash accountability in a given period. Increase the amount of her fidelity bond, should the approved bond is not enough to answer the need of the municipality. Avoid granting cash advances not equal to the net amount of the payrolls for the pay period as provided in the above mentioned regulations. Similarly, liquidate all payroll funds within the prescribed period to avoid risk of loss of fund thru theft or misuse.
observations.managementAction: The LGU adheres strictly to all recommendations in this AAR: Efforts have been made by the Disbursing Officers namely Vicky C. Fuentes & Ponciano Manalo to increase their fidelity bond as official attestation of their cash accountability in every disbursement, and to properly perform their own disbursing functions.

observations.recommendation: Purchases of supplies should be accounted for using the perpetual inventory method as prescribed in Section 114, Volume I, NGAS for LGUs. The Municipal Accountant shall maintain the perpetual inventory records comprising Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC) for each commodity/stock. The General Services Office (GSO) shall likewise maintain stocks cards for supplies and shall consolidate the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued and prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and forward to Accounting unit for the preparation of JEV and record withdrawals in the Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC); and Furthermore, the physical count of inventories shall be conducted on semestral basis, and the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) should be prepared and submitted to this Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year, for the first and second semesters, as prescribed in Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The LGU adheres strictly to all recommendations in this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management must adhere strictly with the regulations set forth under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely review of the documents and communicate the results thereof and avoid undue delay in forwarding the same for technical review and inspection of infra projects. Comply the check-listed requirements for each infrastructure projects listed in Annex H, specifically those which were check-listed together with returned copies of Certificate of Disbursements noted with “Subject to technical review and inspection of projects” in compliance with requirements of COA Circular 2009-1 and the RA 9184, and for sound property management of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The LGU adheres to the recommendations in this AAR. The BAC, GSO and the Municipal Accountant’s office are already in coordination to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: The Management particularly GSO should monitor the renewal of registrations of all vehicles on a yearly basis and their corresponding insurance coverage in order to avoid penalties on account of non-renewal of registrations and deprivation of indemnification due to lack of insurance coverage in case of loss and for sound property management in the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The LGU adheres to the recommendations in this AAR. During exit conference held on February 23, 2017 the management informed the team that they are still completing the documentation pertaining to the registration of the vehicles owned by the municipality. The GSO is having difficulty in completing their documents because some of the OR/CR are with the users of the vehicles and were not turned over to his office.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Legal Officer, Municipal Assessor should facilitate the processing of documents needed for the issuance by the appropriate agency of the Certificate of Title in the name of the LGU as evidence of ownership.
observations.managementAction: During exit conference held on February 23, 2017 the management informed the team that they are still in the process of gathering documents to support the claims of the municipality in establishing legal ownership of their land.
